summary: Russian painter of anti-war works, contemporary of Ilya Repin. Known for The Apotheosis of War (pyramid of skulls in a desert, dedicated 'to all conquerors, past, present and to come'). Part of the Turkestan series.

works.name: The Apotheosis of War (1871)
works.description: Pyramid of skulls in a desert landscape. Dedicated 'to all conquerors, past, present and to come.' Part of the Turkestan series. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Contemporary of Ilya Repin; depicted war in somber, unflinching fashion
examples: This Russian contemporary of Repin depicted war in a rather more sombre and unflinching fashion

comprehensive_summary: Vasily Vereshchagin was a Russian painter of anti-war works, a contemporary of Ilya Repin. He is most known for The Apotheosis of War (1871), depicting a pyramid of skulls in a desert landscape, dedicated 'to all conquerors, past, present and to come.' It is part of his Turkestan series documenting Russian military campaigns in Central Asia.
